purpose: Prioritized benchmark diversification candidates beyond the current recurring MIDAS design file, mapped to phase1 commercialization holdouts.

observed_biases: single recurring MIDAS building file dominates parser and optimization smoke coverage
panel-zone validation is still topology-projected rather than solver-verified
foundation optimization now has live green evidence, but raw-source diversity is still weak
wind raw mapping has live green evidence, but HFFB source diversity is narrow
hinge refresh is green from projected optimization changes, not from richer experimental hysteresis families
megastructure coverage is still synthetic-heavy until a Canton Tower-style real monitoring family is onboarded

gap_targets: P1 raw wind-tunnel HFFB mapping verification
high-rise wind pressure / force benchmark diversification
data_shape: multiple high-rise geometries including interference cases
integration_fit: wind node / floor pressure binding validation
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: add at least one isolated high-rise case and one interference case as external raw wind benchmark fixtures with source manifest
candidates.notes: Best direct match for current wind holdout because it supplies official wind-tunnel pressure and force datasets instead of only semantic pressure binding.

gap_targets: P0 PBD hinge property refresh
member-local hysteresis / damage state benchmark diversification
data_shape: 400+ cyclic lateral-load tests of reinforced concrete columns
geometry, reinforcement, axial load, damage observations
integration_fit: column family holdout split for PBD review package
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: start with a small curated slice of spiral and rectangular columns and convert them into hinge-refresh calibration fixtures
candidates.notes: Most useful external authority source for moving hinge refresh from projected optimization changes toward experimentally anchored response families.

candidates.source_name: DesignSafe liquefaction-induced downdrag on pile foundations dataset
gap_targets: P1 foundation / mat / pile optimization
soil-structure interaction and pile demand diversity
data_shape: centrifuge experiments on liquefaction-induced downdrag in pile foundations
integration_fit: foundation scope promotion beyond below-grade geometric heuristics
SSI regression family for foundation optimization artifact
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: pair one measured DesignSafe centrifuge case with one synthetic SimCenter Pile Group family so measured and parametric foundation tracks are both covered
candidates.notes: Best candidate for diversifying foundation benchmarking because it adds real pile-soil interaction evidence rather than more superstructure-only cases.

gap_targets: P0 panel-zone 3D clash / anchorage
joint shear and anchorage benchmark coverage
data_shape: joint core shear and interface shear-transfer mechanisms
integration_fit: anchorage field naming and minimum row requirements
future solver-verified panel source acceptance tests
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: use as the first benchmark-backed schema reference for panel-zone joint rows and anchorage failure annotations
candidates.notes: This is not a drop-in raw dataset like TPU or SPD, but it is the strongest official reference for the exact mechanisms the current panel holdout is missing.

candidates.source_name: USGS National Strong Motion Project structural arrays
gap_targets: measured building-response holdout diversity
building instrumentation validation beyond one recurring design file
data_shape: instrumented structures with ground and structural channels
integration_fit: external measured benchmark family beyond Atwood-only recurrence
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: curate one mid-rise building array and one taller instrumented building response example for measured-vs-modeled transfer validation
candidates.notes: Best source for widening measured-response holdouts without tying the pipeline to another single proprietary design model.

candidates.source_name: PEER Report 1999-11 concrete bridge component performance database
gap_targets: bridge / civil benchmark diversification
component-level performance limits
data_shape: bridge component and system performance library
integration_fit: civil/railway expansion beyond current tunnel and track examples
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: stage as a secondary civil benchmark track after wind, hinge, and foundation diversification land
candidates.notes: Useful for civil expansion, but it should come after the four commercialization holdouts have wider direct evidence.

gap_targets: measured megatall dynamic benchmark beyond Atwood recurrence
reduced-order SHM benchmark ingestion for irregular/megatall review chain
viewer/results explorer provenance on official measured high-rise response
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: ingest as an official reduced-order SHM benchmark, not as a full-order native FE benchmark
candidates.notes: Best next-frontier benchmark for the current repo because it exercises measured megatall response, provenance, and viewer/review chains without requiring full-order FE ingestion on day one.

gap_targets: authority-grade nonlinear / collapse benchmark diversity
member-local hysteresis and frame-response validation beyond projected hinge refresh
blind prediction style compare/report/viewer workflow
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: start with one PEER/E-Defense benchmark family that can be normalized into source_manifest -> benchmark_case -> compare_report -> viewer_entry, before attempting broader collapse suites
candidates.notes: Very high value, but should follow Canton Tower because the repo is already strongest on measured/review chains and only partially closed on full collapse mechanics.

gap_targets: SSI / containment benchmark hardening
impedance and transfer-function validation for heavy stiff structures
future thick-shell / stronger contact breadth validation
data_shape: SSI target curves and response summaries
possible model metadata, but raw FE meshes may be unavailable
candidates.recommended_ingestion_mode: treat as a deferred reference benchmark until a trusted public model package or directly ingestible benchmark dataset is secured
candidates.notes: Important long-term, but not the right immediate benchmark because current public evidence looks stronger at the report/reference level than at raw ingestible FE package level.
